channel.title: BBC News
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Minister defends Translink fares freeze despite possibility of cuts to services
item.description: Cuts to services across Metro, Ulsterbus and NI Railways are being explored by Translink under savings plans.

item.title: Fire burning at recycling site in east Belfast
item.description: Police and the fire service are advising motorists to avoid the East Twin Road area and to keep windows and doors shut.

item.title: Man dies after single-vehicle collision involving motorbike
item.description: Michael Kenna, 39, from Belturbet in County Cavan was pronounced dead at the scene on Sunday morning.

item.title: Tyrone GAA legend McGuigan dies aged 71
item.description: Former Tyrone forward Frank McGuigan, widely regarded as one of the county's finest footballers, dies at the age of 71.

item.title: What do the Irish by-election results mean for Sinn Féin?
item.description: For Mary Lou McDonald's party, it was yet another bad weekend of bad election results.

item.title: Deported flotilla activist from Belfast returns home
item.description: Fra Hughes was one of hundreds of activists who were detained by Israel while trying to bring aid to Gaza.

item.title: Counting resumes in Galway West by-election
item.description: Two by-elections are being held to fill the vacant Dáil seats in Galway West and Dublin Central.

item.title: 'Frustrating' MOT backlogs as test centres opening dates unconfirmed
item.description: Opening dates for new test centres in Hydebank and Mallusk are still not confirmed despite both being due to have opened years ago.

item.title: Landmark building to disappear from city centre
item.description: From bankers, balls, books to bombs, this site may no longer be original but it does have history.

item.title: Couple left 'badly shaken' after being tied up during burglary
item.description: Two masked men entered the property on the Fermoyle Road around 01:15 BST on Saturday and tied the couple up.

item.title: Fire causes significant damage to football clubhouse
item.description: NI Fire Service gets Ballinamallard FC club room fire under control

item.title: Five arrested over dissident republican activity released
item.description: Five men were arrested in Poleglass then questioned in Musgrave Police Station.

item.title: First Chelsea Flower Show garden relocated to NI
item.description: A gold-medal winning garden is being gifted to community volunteers who run a foodbank in Strabane.

item.title: Deported Irish flotilla activists return from Israel
item.description: Irish participants in Global Sumud Flotilla bringing aid to Gaza were deported to Ireland.

item.title: Woman jailed for assisting offender over murder of Kathryn Parton
item.description: Suzanne Love admitted perverting the course of justice by removing items from a crime scene and washing items with intent to destroy evidence.

item.title: Sunday trading extension a 'cop-out' say business leaders
item.description: A council committee recommended earlier opening should be allowed during the all-Ireland music and dance festival - Fleadh Cheoil na hÉireann – in August.

item.title: NI hospices to receive £1.6m one-off payment for palliative care
item.description: The DoH has now pledged £1.1m of funding for NI's four hospices, and £500,000 for the children's hospice.

item.title: Police believe man was attacked with brush shaft before death
item.description: The 51-year-old was attacked when a number of intruders entered his apartment on the Holywood Road.

item.title: UUP leader denies intervening over Doug Beattie candidacy
item.description: Last week it emerged Beattie was not expected to be chosen as the party's candidate for Upper Bann in next year's assembly election.

item.title: Pope Leo has 'genuine desire' for end of wars - Irish PM
item.description: Taoiseach Micheál Martin and his wife Mary were greeted in the San Damaso Courtyard on Friday morning before meeting the Pope in the Papal Library.

item.title: King Charles plays ukulele on final day of NI trip
item.description: This is his 43rd visit to Northern Ireland, first as Prince of Wales and now as King.

item.title: 'My brother told police Creswell was a killer, but they ignored him'
item.description: The brother of Paul Lusby, who tried to alert police about the man accused of killing Katie Simpson, wants a public inquiry into the police investigation.

item.title: 'The care system failed my daughter twice'
item.description: A woman whose daughter was treated at two facilities, including Muckamore Abbey Hospital, says she feels lessons haven't been learned.

item.title: 'I had no fears about robotic surgery after my cancer diagnosis'
item.description: William Duncan is the first person in Northern Ireland to have his oesophageal cancer removed with help from a robot.

item.title: Are letters to 'dodgy stick' viewers a new anti-piracy approach?
item.description: Sky has issued about 200 cease-and-desist letters to alleged users in the Republic of Ireland.

item.title: Fergie's former racehorse enjoying a retirement with beaches and beer
item.description: Spirit Dancer had a long career but he is now enjoying beach walks and beer for dinner.

item.title: Good jobs or bad law?: The row over workers' rights
item.description: The Good Jobs Bill was first announced almost two years ago but the legal text of the draft law has still not been published.

item.title: Pharmacists 'can't afford' to keep providing blister packs
item.description: Community Pharmacy NI says "many pharmacy teams are at capacity".

item.title: BBC Newsline signed summary
item.description: A signed summary of BBC Newsline.

item.title: What do young people think of a social media ban?
item.description: UK online safety minister Kanishka Narayan says any ban on social media for under-16s agreed at Westminster will apply in NI.

item.title: 'What a memory' say Race Across the World pair
item.description: Molly Clifford and her father Andrew from Maghera travelled from Italy to Mongolia in the competition.

item.title: Private dog poo patrollers called in to clean up streets
item.description: One Northern Ireland council has turned to a private company to hand out fines.

item.title: 'Dementia reading group makes my life easier'
item.description: Research carried out by Queen's University and Dementia NI suggests there are benefits to reading in a group.

item.title: Bands drum up young people to challenge stereotypes
item.description: The project from the Belfast Bands Forum aims to introduce young people to positive cultural expression through music.

item.title: 'If I could tell 14-year-old me I'd be playing Maud in Rivals'
item.description: Irish actress Victoria Smurfit plays Maud O'Hara in the Disney+ hit series Rivals.

item.title: Childhood trauma common theme in documentary - Frampton
item.description: It is the first time television cameras have been given this sort of access and the result is Carl Frampton: On Probation.

item.title: Thousands expected for 2026 Foyle Maritime Festival
item.description: The four-day event in June will see the River Foyle transformed into a major celebration of Derry's maritime history.

item.title: Man escapes injury in arson attack on house
item.description: Police are investigating if a petrol bomb was thrown through the window of the house in Coleraine, County Londonderry.

item.title: Councillor has pro-Palestinian rally charge dropped
item.description: A judge said Shaun Harkin had no case to answer but a case against four other defendants will continue.

item.title: 'Collusive behaviour' reports must include line that police ombudsman exceeded powers
item.description: Reports which found collusive behaviour by RUC officers in a series of loyalist murders must include a notice about the watchdog exceeding its legal powers.

item.title: Children involved in 'vile' race-hate harassment, says councillor
item.description: Sinn Féin councillor Alex Duffy condemned the continued harassment of a family in Strathfoyle, near Derry.

item.title: One person dies in two-car crash
item.description: Police say one person was pronounced dead at the scene following the two-car crash in Omagh on Sunday.

item.title: Coleraine left 'vulnerable' in university job losses
item.description: Ulster University announced last month that it is set to make up to 450 staff redundant as it needs to make savings of about £25m.

item.title: To go or not to go: Should pupils be allowed to use the toilet when they want?
item.description: Undignified waiting vs disruption in class and vaping in the toilets - young people and teachers tackle a debate.

item.title: How I fell victim to 'ghost' insurance broker scam
item.description: Amie Donaghey is among thousands of victims of "ghost brokers" who sell fake insurance on social media.

item.title: Campaigner condemns race-hate harassment of family
item.description: The police say they have spoken to a number of young people about the harassment of the family in Strathfoyle, near Derry.

item.title: BBC 'remain committed' to Radio Foyle after building listed for sale
item.description: The building on Northland Road, which the BBC operates through a lease agreement, appeared on PropertyPal.

item.title: MP says graffiti seeks to hurt Bloody Sunday families
item.description: Eastwood says the offensive graffiti on Free Derry Wall was designed to traumatise the families of Bloody Sunday victims.

item.title: Man found guilty of attempting to kill police officers
item.description: Charlie Love was also found guilty of causing an explosion likely to endanger life and possessing explosives.

item.title: Stadium plan dashed as special school set to expand
item.description: A site in Strabane earmarked for a new football ground has been bought to allow a special school to expand.

item.title: Gaza flotilla activists deported from Israel as backlash over treatment grows
item.description: A video showing a far-right Israeli minister taunting activists who were kneeling with their hands tied behind their backs has been widely condemned.

item.title: Man jailed for fatal Boxing Day hit-and-run
item.description: John Halpin pleaded guilty to dangerous driving causing the deaths of a married couple, Anthony Hogg and Georgina Hogg Moore.

item.title: Protesters gather outside Ireland's parliament over Yves Sakila's 'disturbing' death
item.description: Yves Sakila, 35, died on one of Dublin's busiest shopping streets after an alleged shoplifting incident.

item.title: Irish foreign minister slams treatment of detainees by Israel
item.description: In a video on social media, Israel's national security minister is shown taunting detainees.

item.title: Up to 350 jobs under threat at Meta in Ireland
item.description: Meta has submitted a collective redundancy notification to Ireland's Department of Enterprise.

item.title: Irish president finishes first official visit to GB
item.description: Catherine Connolly spent the day at the University of Leeds and the Leeds Irish Centre.

item.title: Israeli detention of President Connolly's sister 'unacceptable' - Irish PM
item.description: Dr Margaret Connolly is among 12 Irish citizens on board the aid flotilla which was boarded by Israeli forces.

item.title: Praise for inspirational boy who saved family from drowning
item.description: The 13-year-old Australian boy who swam for hours to get help for his family after they were swept out to sea in Australia has met with the Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin earlier today.

item.title: Irish president continues first official visit to GB
item.description: It is President Connolly's first official visit to Great Britain since her inauguration in November.

item.title: Body of a man discovered in Donegal residence
item.description: The body currently remains at the scene, as Garda forensic and technical examinations are carried out.

item.title: Man's conviction quashed for 2018 murder in County Louth
item.description: Aaron Connolly had denied murdering 18-year-old Cameron Reilly on a night out in May 2018.

item.title: Donegal crashes claim three lives
item.description: Three men, Two in their 20s and one in his 30s, have died following separate crashes in County Donegal.

item.title: Pedestrian dies after being hit by car
item.description: Gardaí (Irish police) said they responded after a pedestrian was struck by a car on the N11 Bray Road, Loughlinstown at about 23:50 local time.

item.title: Man arrested after north Dublin house death released
item.description: Police and emergency services responded to a call in north Dublin shortly after 00:30 local time on Saturday.

item.title: Man in 60s dies following tractor crash
item.description: The single-vehicle incident happened on the L3355 near Mullaghycullen, Drumcong, on Sunday.

item.title: Man dies following alleged shoplifting incident
item.description: Security personnel detained the man in connection with the alleged incident at a store on Friday.

item.title: Cannabis worth over €515k seized at Dublin Airport
item.description: The drugs were seized by revenue officers after a passenger's baggage was searched on Friday.

item.title: Around the island in 48 days: White-tailed eagle goes on Irish grand tour
item.description: The bird, which hatched in Glengarriff Nature Reserve in County Cork last year, began its journey from its birthplace on March 22, 2026.

item.title: Ex-Irish PM Ahern clarifies immigration stance after 'I worry about Africans' recording
item.description: Bertie Ahern says he did not know he was being recorded after a video emerged of him discussing African and Muslim immigrants into Ireland.
